audit_items.proof_trace: All externally reachable pointer dereferences in search.c are guarded: `ber` is checked for NULL (line 122), `base` and `filter` have NULL fallbacks (lines 295-302, 338-340), and `ber_printf` 'v' format handles NULL attrs internally (liblber/encode.c:590). The missing NULL checks on output parameters `msgidp` (line 128) and `res` (lines 172, 403, 433) require application callers to violate API contracts by passing NULL; all actual callers in the codebase pass valid stack addresses. The assert-only guards in `ldap_bv2escaped_filter_value_x` (lines 503-504) are disabled in NDEBUG builds but all call sites pass valid non-NULL struct berval pointers.
